judgment.summary: A large, profitable company with strong cash flow and analyst support, but facing modest growth and significant debt.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 9.5%; next earnings date is 2026-07-28.
positives: Forward P/E 18.44x — attractive relative to trailing P/E and analyst growth estimates.
Free cash flow $17.69B TTM — substantial cash generation supports operations and dividends.
Analyst consensus is 'buy' with a mean target of $390.35, suggesting upside potential.
Dividend yield 2.3% — provides a steady income stream for shareholders.
negatives: Revenue growth 2.0% YoY and earnings growth 0.7% YoY — indicate slowing top-line and bottom-line expansion.
Quarterly earnings growth -0.2% — recent performance shows a slight decline.
Total debt $77.92B vs $31.23B cash — significant leverage on the balance sheet.
Current ratio 0.80 and quick ratio 0.72 — suggest potential short-term liquidity concerns.

news.paragraph: UnitedHealth Group is seeing positive momentum as its managed care segment outpaces the broader economy. This suggests underlying strength in its core business operations, which could translate to continued stable performance. Investors will be watching the upcoming earnings report for further confirmation of this trend.

headlines.title: UnitedHealth Group (UNH) Gains as Managed Care Outgrows Economy
